parameters.content: wf_release_channel_execute performs a dry-run or live release channel execution via /release/channel-execute. It stays honest about unsupported channels by producing operator-facing handoff artifacts instead of pretending to publish through fantasy APIs.

parameters.jsCode: return [{json:{tenant_id:'default', release_version:'', channel_names:[], include_publication_bundle:true, persist:false, dry_run:true, execute_webhooks:false}}];

const body = $json.body || $json; return [{json:{status: body.status || 'ok', release_version: body.release_version, publication_ready: !!body.publication_ready, bundle_path: body.bundle_path || null, count: body.count || 0, delivered_count: body.delivered_count || 0, prepared_count: body.prepared_count || 0, blocked_count: body.blocked_count || 0, items: (body.execution_items || []).map(item => ({channel_name:item.channel_name, execution_mode:item.execution_mode, status:item.status, delivery_ref:item.delivery_ref || null, output_path:item.output_path || null, blocking_reasons:item.blocking_reasons || []})), next_actions: body.next_actions || []}}];
